mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, market participants price in imminent recessionary tail risks and corporate earnings downgrades, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 8.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during periods of stable inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization without triggering monetary tightening, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative when industrial production rises alongside stable CPI, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 36-month windows, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of positive payroll growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the consumer price index above 3.0 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and stagnant retail sales indicates supply-side bottlenecks rather than consumer weakness, prompting market repricing toward positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of elevated unemployment confirms a broad economic contraction, triggering defensive portfolio shifts and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to achieve a statistically significant negative alpha in out-of-sample testing.
